metadata.summary: Asynchronous Python framework to build TeamSpeak 3 Server Query bots

documentation:
# TSBot   [![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/tsbot)](https://pypi.org/project/tsbot/)

Asynchronous Python framework to build **TeamSpeak 3 Server Query** bots

## ✅ Features

- Modern Python `async` and `await` syntax
- Secure connection through SSH
- Ease of use query building
- Built-in and configurable ratelimiter if no access to `query_ip_allowlist.txt`

## ✏️ Examples

```python
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio

from tsbot import TSBot, TSCtx, query


bot = TSBot(
    username="USERNAME",
    password="PASSWORD",
    address="ADDRESS",
)


@bot.command("hello")
async def hello_world(bot: TSBot, ctx: TSCtx):
    await bot.respond(ctx, f"Hello {ctx['invokername']}!")


@bot.on("cliententerview")
async def poke_on_enter(bot: TSBot, ctx: TSCtx):
    poke_query = query("clientpoke").params(clid=ctx["clid"], msg="Welcome to the server!")
    await bot.send(poke_query)


asyncio.run(bot.run())
```

**Check out [📁examples](https://github.com/jykob/TSBot/blob/main/examples) for more**

## 📦 Installation

**Python 3.10 or higher is required**

Installing with pip:

```shell
# Linux/macOS
python3 -m pip install tsbot

# Windows
py -3 -m pip install tsbot
```

methods.name: tsbot.default_plugins.keepalive.KeepAlive._keep_alive_task
documentation:
Task to keep connection alive with the TeamSpeak server

Normally TeamSpeak server cuts the connection to the query client
after 5 minutes of inactivity. If the bot doesn't send any commands
to the server, this task sends a command to keep connection alive.

class_variables.name: tsbot.default_plugins.keepalive.KeepAlive.KEEP_ALIVE_INTERVAL
documentation: 4 minutes

functions.name: tsbot.parsers._parse_quoted_arg
documentation:
Parses a quoted argument, returns it and unparsed part.

If a quote doesn't have a whitespace behind it, that part is not considered a quote end.
If a valid quote is not found, parse as normal argument.

functions.name: tsbot.parsers._parse_arg
documentation: Parse an argument out of unparsed message, return it and unparsed part.

functions.name: tsbot.parsers._parse_kwarg
documentation: Parse an key-value argument

functions.name: tsbot.parsers.parse_args_kwargs
documentation: Parses a message in to arguments and keyword arguments

functions.name: tsbot.parsers.split_ensure_splits
documentation: Splits a string at least maxsplit times, filling the rest with fill value

classes.name: tsbot.exceptions.TSException
documentation: Exception related to TSBot.

classes.name: tsbot.exceptions.TSResponseError
documentation: Raised when a response from server has error_id set to other than 0.

classes.name: tsbot.exceptions.TSResponsePermissionError
documentation:
Raised when a response has error_id of '2568', indicating that the client
doesn't have the proper permissions to execute this query.

classes.name: tsbot.exceptions.TSCommandError
documentation:
Command handlers can raise this exception to indicate
that something went wrong while running the handler.

classes.name: tsbot.exceptions.TSPermissionError
documentation:
Command handlers can raise this exception to indicate
that the user running this command doesn't have the
proper permissions.

classes.name: tsbot.exceptions.TSInvalidParameterError
documentation:
Raised when a call to a command handler doesn't match the signature
of the handler.

functions.name: tsbot.encoders.escape
documentation: Escapes characters that need escaping according to ESCAPE_MAP

functions.name: tsbot.encoders.unescape
documentation: Undo escaping of characters according to ESCAPE_MAP

methods.name: tsbot.events.event.TSEvent.from_server_notification
documentation:
Creates a TSEvent instance from server notify

Will remove the 'notify' from the beginning of the 'event'

methods.name: tsbot.events.manager.EventManager.handle_events_task
documentation: Task to run events put into the event queue.

methods.name: tsbot.events.manager.EventManager.register_event_handler
documentation: Registers event handlers that will be called when given event happens.

classes.name: tsbot.query_builder.builder.TSQuery

methods.name: tsbot.query_builder.builder.TSQuery.__init__
documentation:
---
param command: Base query command.
param options: Options attached to the query.
param parameter_blocks: Parameter blocks attached to the query.
param parameters: Parameters attached to the query.
---

methods.name: tsbot.query_builder.builder.TSQuery.option
documentation:
Add options to the command eg. `-groups`.

```
param args

Tuple of options to be attached.

return

New  instance with added params
```

methods.name: tsbot.query_builder.builder.TSQuery.params
documentation:
Add parameters to the command eg. `cldbid=12`.

```
param kwargs

Keyword to be attached.

return

New  instance with added parameters
```

methods.name: tsbot.query_builder.builder.TSQuery.param_block
documentation:
Add parameter blocks eg. `clid=1 | clid=2 | clid=3` to the command.
Takes in either an iterable of parameters in form of dict[str, Stringable]
or **one** block at a time.

```
param blocks

Iterable of parameter blocks to be attached.

param kwargs

Parameters to be attached to single block.

return

New  instance with added parameter blocks
```

methods.name: tsbot.query_builder.builder.TSQuery.compile
documentation:
Compiles the query into a raw command.

```
return

The compiled query command.
```
Class to represent query commands to the TeamSpeak server.

methods.name: tsbot.commands.manager.CommandManager.handle_command_event
documentation: Logic to handle commands

classes.name: tsbot.commands.command.TSCommand
documentation: TODO: Use after 'typing_extensions' releases support for Python 3.10 'Concatenate[...]'
CommandHandler = Callable[Concatenate["bot.TSBot", "context.TSCtx", ...], Coroutine[None, None, None]]

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.ssh_connection.SSHConnection.authenticate
documentation: Teamspeak SSH query clients are already authenticated on connect

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.connect
documentation:
Establishes connection to the TeamSpeak server.

Once awaited, the connection should be ready to write and receive data.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.validate_header
documentation:
Validate the header given by the server.

This method shall consume the whole header to make
sure normal communication with the server can be started.
Will raise `ConnectionAbortedError` if invalid header found.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.authenticate
documentation:
Authenticate the connection.

This method is called after the header is validated.
Normal communication with the server allowed.

Will raise `ConnectionAbortedError` if cannot authenticate connection.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.close
documentation: Closes the connection to the server.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.wait_closed
documentation: Awaits until the connection is closed.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.write
documentation:
Write data to the server.

This method must terminate data with line ending.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection_types.abc.Connection.readline
documentation:
Reads a single line determined by line ending sequence.

Will include line ending sequence in return str

methods.name: tsbot.response.TSResponse.first
documentation: First datapoint from the response

methods.name: tsbot.response.TSResponse.last
documentation: Last datapoint from the response
Class to represent the response to a query from a Teamspeak server.

methods.name: tsbot.connection.connection.TSConnection._connection_handler

Task to handle connection.

Will try to upkeep connection specified by the config.

PluginCommandHandler = Callable[
Concatenate[_TP, "bot.TSBot", "context.TSCtx", ...], Coroutine[None, None, None]

classes.name: tsbot.plugin.TSPlugin
documentation: Base class for plugins

methods.name: tsbot.bot.TSBot.__init__
documentation:
---
param address: Address of the TeamSpeak server.
param connection_retries: The amount of attempts on each connection.
param connection_retry_timeout: The period between each connection attempt.
param invoker: Command indicator.
param nickname: Display name for the bot client.
param password: Generated password for the query account.
param port: Port of the SSH connection.
param protocol: Type of connection to be used.
param query_timeout: Timeout for query commands.
param ratelimit_calls: Calls per period.
param ratelimit_period: Period interval.
param ratelimited: If the connection should be ratelimited.
param server_id: Id of the virtual server.
param username: Login name of the query account.
---

methods.name: tsbot.bot.TSBot.send
documentation:
Sends queries to the server, assuring only one of them gets sent at a time.

```
param query

Instance of  to be send to the server.

return

Response from the server.
```

methods.name: tsbot.bot.TSBot.send_raw
documentation:
Sends raw commands to the server.

Its recommended to use built-in query builder and
{func}`send()<tsbot.TSBot.send()>` method instead.

```
param raw_query

Raw query command to be send to the server.

return

Response from the server.
```

methods.name: tsbot.bot.TSBot.close
documentation:
Method to close the bot.

Emits `close` event to notify that the client is closing,
cancels background tasks and send quit command.

methods.name: tsbot.bot.TSBot._update_bot_info
documentation: Update useful information about the bot instance

methods.name: tsbot.bot.TSBot.run
documentation:
Run the bot.

Connects to the server, registers the server to send events
to the bot and schedules background tasks.

Awaits until the bot disconnects.
